QUESTION: Is it yellow when ripe?

content.thinking:
The question is asking if mango is yellow when ripe. Mangoes can be various colors when ripe - they can be yellow, but they can also be red, orange, green, or a combination of colors depending on the variety. Some mangoes remain green even when ripe, while others turn yellow, red, or orange.

However, the question asks "Is it yellow when ripe?" - many common mango varieties do turn yellow or have yellow coloring when ripe. This is a characteristic of many (though not all) mangoes. Since yellow is a common color for ripe mangoes, I should answer "yes" even though not all mangoes are yellow when ripe.

Actually, I need to be more careful. The question is whether it is "typically" or generally yellow when ripe. Many mangoes are yellow when ripe, but many are also red/orange or even green. This is a bit ambiguous. However, yellow is certainly one of the common colors for ripe mangoes, and many popular varieties are yellow when ripe.

I think "yes" is the most appropriate answer since yellow is a very common color for ripe mangoes.

A mango is NOT a citrus fruit. Citrus fruits include oranges, lemons, limes, grapefruits, etc. Mangoes belong to the family Anacardiaceae, not Rutaceae (the citrus family).

So the answer should be "no".
